title: Connections, Channels, Heartbeats, Errors, and Recovery · Compiler Case: Heartbeat Timeout
summary: A service opens one TCP connection per message, shares one channel across unrelated concurrent owners, disables heartbeats, retries instantly, and redeclares consumers before topology or credentials recover. Planned artifact: Add and verify the heartbeat timeout increment in connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure.
Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Heartbeat Timeout · Theory Heartbeat Timeout
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7950 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound heartbeat and reconnect policy while rejecting stale channel-generation callbacks; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Heartbeat Timeout contract for the supplied data.
